blurb: Apple developer kits apply uniform security and privacy protections to third-party applications using those frameworks. All APIs accessing sensitive hardware or user data – including ARKit, HealthKit, HomeKit, and SiriKit – require explicit user permission grants via the TCC framework. Sensitive processing occurs in system-managed processes rather than within the app's address space, limiting what a compromised app can extract. Extensions used by frameworks like ReplayKit and SiriKit run as separate processes with minimal privileges, preserving the security benefits of the extension model throughout the developer kit ecosystem while enabling powerful platform capabilities.
